mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative direction in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization that improves short-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below baseline during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer prices forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, tightening financial conditions and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal shows no significant predictive power (t-stat < 1.96) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market that sustains wage growth and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of rising housing start periods, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.03 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly jump in industrial production indicates an immediate supply-side acceleration that boosts short-term earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of threshold events, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.
